skill.name: devops-quality
skill.description: Code quality standards, linting rules, and CI/CD principles.
skill.summary: Code quality standards, linting rules, and CI/CD principles.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external commands, environment file access, and weak cryptography, but review found no executable code or malicious intent. The command-like text is Markdown documentation, the .env reference says secrets should never be committed, and SKILL.md:3 contains no cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time when linting, testing, and release checks are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise guidance for code quality and CI/CD standards.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes linting and formatting expectations using ESLint.
References TypeScript and Next.js configuration files to check during review.
Recommends feature branch workflow and semantic commit messages.
Suggests CI checks for linting and builds on pull requests.
Identifies Jest and Playwright as preferred testing tools.
Documents basic local and production secrets handling practices.
limitations: Does not provide executable scripts or automation code.
Does not include complete ESLint, Jest, or Playwright configuration files.
Does not define deployment steps beyond high-level CI/CD guidance.
Does not validate repository-specific settings without additional project context.

use_cases.title: Standardize Pull Request Checks
use_cases.description: Create a short checklist for linting, build verification, tests, and branch hygiene before review.

use_cases.title: Guide New Contributors
use_cases.description: Explain the expected development workflow, commit style, and quality checks for a project.

use_cases.title: Plan CI Improvements
use_cases.description: Turn the documented lint and build requirements into a practical GitHub Actions implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Quality Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-quality skill to summarize the code quality rules I should follow in this repository.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PR Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-quality skill to create a pull request checklist for linting, build checks, tests, commits, and secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Repository Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-quality skill to compare this repository with the documented linting, testing, and CI expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Design a CI Adoption Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-quality skill to propose a phased GitHub Actions plan for linting, builds, unit tests, and E2E tests.

output_examples.input: Create a quality checklist for a pull request.
output: Add unit or E2E tests where the change affects behavior.
Verify that no local environment files are committed.

output_examples.input: Explain how we should improve CI for this project.
output_examples.output: A useful CI plan would start with lint and build checks on every pull request. Add unit tests next, then include Playwright coverage for authentication and other critical user flows.

output_examples.input: What should a new contributor know before opening a PR?
output_examples.output: A contributor should use a feature branch, follow semantic commit style, run linting, check TypeScript rules, and keep local secrets out of version control.

best_practices: Use this skill with repository context so recommendations match the actual toolchain.
Ask for checklists that separate required checks from future improvements.
Pair the guidance with real CI logs or configuration files when diagnosing failures.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as a complete CI implementation.
Do not use it to justify committing local environment files.
Do not assume the named tools are installed without checking the repository.

faq.question: Does this skill run linting or tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance about linting, builds, testing, and CI/CD practices.

faq.question: Which linting tool does it reference?
faq.answer: It references ESLint and points to common configuration files for review.

faq.question: Does it include GitHub Actions workflow files?
faq.answer: No. It recommends GitHub Actions as a future CI option but does not include workflow code.

faq.question: Can it help with commit message standards?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends semantic commits and can help create review checklists.

faq.question: How does it handle secrets?
faq.answer: It advises keeping local .env files out of version control and using production environment variables.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill contains documentation only and no executable code.
